diagnosis: The founder has direct community and sales access but limited engineering capacity, so the first opportunity should be testable as a manual workflow before software is built.
assumptions: The founder can recruit at least five community operators for interviews.
The technical teammate is available only after a direction passes an initial demand test.

evidence.claim: The founder has sales and community skills but limited technical capacity.
evidence.summary: The claim comes directly from the user's stated skills and constraints.

directions.name: Community progress and proof workflow
directions.painful_job: Track member progress and produce credible completion evidence without maintaining several spreadsheets.
directions.first_test: Manually produce one weekly progress report for three community operators using their existing exports.
unknowns: Whether operators will share the required data
What they currently pay in staff time

directions.name: Local business lead follow-up service
directions.painful_job: Follow up with inbound leads consistently without adding another full-time employee.
directions.first_test: Run a concierge follow-up workflow for two businesses and measure response and booking rates.
unknowns: Willingness to pay for managed follow-up

directions.name: Founder evidence profile
directions.customer: Early founders preparing for mentors, grants, or accelerators
directions.painful_job: Keep project proof, feedback, and milestones in a form that reviewers can verify quickly.
directions.first_test: Create five profiles manually and ask three reviewers whether the format shortens review time.
unknowns: Whether existing profile tools already solve the workflow

recommendation.rationale: Test the community progress workflow first because the founder's distribution access makes recruitment faster and a concierge version can produce evidence without engineering.
recommendation.success_threshold: At least three of five suitable operators describe the workflow as recurring and two agree to provide data for a manual pilot.
recommendation.kill_or_pivot_condition: Pause or switch direction if fewer than two operators will provide data or if the current spreadsheet workflow takes less than one staff-hour per week.

experiments.name: Community operator workflow test
experiments.assumption: Community operators have a recurring and costly progress-reporting problem.
experiments.method: Interview five operators, observe their current reporting workflow, and ask qualified participants for a one-week data-assisted pilot.
experiments.metric: Observed staff time and number of operators agreeing to a pilot
experiments.threshold: Three report at least two staff-hours per week and two begin a pilot

sources_checked.note: Used the founder's stated skills, team, and time constraints.

sources_not_checked.note: No community archive was connected for this example, so local demand remains unverified.
Comparable product research should follow only after the first customer workflow is selected.

next_actions.action: Recruit five community operators who currently produce progress reports.
Observe the current workflow and record frequency, staff time, errors, and current tools.
next_actions.done_when: Every interview has the same four behavioral fields recorded.

next_actions.action: Offer a one-week manual report pilot to operators who meet the pain threshold.
next_actions.done_when: At least two operators provide data or the direction reaches its kill condition.
